steps.title: Use the sine ratio
steps.content:
In a right triangle, sine compares the side opposite the angle to the hypotenuse. If $o$ is the opposite side and $h$ is the hypotenuse, then
$$\sin y^{\circ}=\frac{o}{h}$$

steps.title: Substitute the side lengths
steps.content:
For angle $y^{\circ}$, the opposite side is $5$ and the hypotenuse is $26$:
$$\sin(y^{\circ})=\frac{5}{26}$$
The answer is A.
